hierarchy.h1: text-4xl sm:text-5xl lg:text-6xl font-semibold tracking-tight leading-none

spacing_and_layout.general: Generous spacing. Use p-6, p-8, p-12. Avoid cramped containers.
spacing_and_layout.border_radius: rounded-2xl for large cards, rounded-xl for standard cards, rounded-full for buttons/badges.
spacing_and_layout.grids: Use 'Control Room' grid for Dashboard (grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4, gap-6). Use 'Bento' for marketing/landing (gap-8).

components.buttons: Primary: bg-brand-primary text-white hover:bg-brand-primary-hover rounded-full px-6 py-3 transition-colors duration-200. Accent (Golden): bg-brand-accent text-white hover:bg-brand-accent-hover rounded-full.
components.cards: bg-white rounded-2xl p-6 border border-border-default shadow-sm hover:shadow-md transition-shadow duration-200.
components.inputs: bg-white border border-border-default rounded-xl px-4 py-3 focus:ring-2 focus:ring-brand-primary/20 focus:border-brand-primary outline-none transition-all.

images.description: Chef carefully prepares plates, perfect for restaurant dashboard hero or onboarding.
Golden oil pouring, suitable for 'volume collected' impact metrics or collector dashboard.
Macro photography of green leaf, use for ecological impact dashboard sections.

media.icons: Use lucide-react (already available) for all system icons. Use leaf, MapPin, Droplet, Truck, TrendingUp.

instructions_to_main_agent.tech_stack: React, Tailwind CSS, Shadcn/UI (though user mentioned pure HTML/JS, follow system constraint using React/Tailwind but keep the UI looking clean and simple).
pages_to_build: 2. Restaurant Dashboard (Volume entry, Publish Availability, Impact Metrics)
3. Collector Dashboard (Interactive Map of available oil points, Route builder, Confirmation)
ux_rules: Ensure strong contrast. Text colors over brand-primary should be white.
Collector map should occupy significant space (Control Room grid style).
Use 'data-testid' on all interactive elements (e.g., data-testid='login-button', data-testid='publish-oil-btn').
Do NOT use heavy card components unnecessarily; go for flat rectangular shapes with subtle borders for widgets.
